probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increased from 10% to 12%: compound stress approaching ELEVATED with two legs at/near thresholds, Extreme Fear persisting, XRP at $1.41 (was $1.35 on March 3 — slight recovery but still weak). Base case increased from 40% to 42% at the expense of mid case (38% to 34%): the macro deterioration makes the mid-case price targets harder to reach even as regulatory/institutional signals improve. Bull unchanged at 12% — the regulatory acceleration and Ripple Prime expansion support the upside case but macro headwinds limit near-term probability shift. The regulatory environment is the best it's been, but the market isn't pricing it due to macro stress.

bear_case.score_reasoning: The bear case draws significant weight from three factors: (1) ODL volume data remains completely absent — the core settlement utility metric is unverifiable, (2) RLUSD's XRPL-native concentration is only 22% ($355M of $1.589B), undermining the XRPL-specific settlement thesis, and (3) macro stress is approaching compound breach while XRP trades at $1.41, well below thesis price targets. Regulatory wins are real but haven't translated to on-chain metrics.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT continues to serve 11,500+ banks globally. GPI upgrades reduce settlement times. The tokenized securities interagency FAQ doesn't displace SWIFT's messaging monopoly — it enables parallel rails.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new signals of expansion into XRP's target corridors. Visa focused on consumer/merchant rather than institutional settlement infrastructure.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Kinexys remains the most credible direct competitor for institutional blockchain settlement. JPMorgan's balance sheet and client relationships give it structural advantages Ripple cannot match on distribution alone.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: BIS Nexus aims to connect domestic fast payment systems without requiring bridge currencies. If successful, this directly undermines XRP's core value proposition as a bridge asset. No definitive deployment timeline but central bank backing gives it credibility.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ondo Finance pushing 30+ sector tokenization on Ethereum, and $1.234B of RLUSD is on Ethereum, not XRPL. The interagency FAQ treating permissionless blockchains equally benefits Ethereum at least as much as XRPL. Ethereum's institutional tokenization footprint exceeds XRPL's by a significant margin.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data has been unavailable for an extended period with no timeline for restoration. This is the single most important metric for the settlement thesis and its absence is itself a bearish signal — if volumes were impressive, Ripple would publish them.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: No token velocity data available. XRP daily volume of $2.66B against $86B market cap suggests moderate velocity, but without hold-time data or institutional vs speculative flow breakdown, the utility-to-speculation ratio remains unknown.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Fear & Greed at 22, compound stress approaching ELEVATED, Section 122 tariffs active, and DXY at 99.04 (weak dollar unusual during tariff regime) all suggest markets pricing meaningful growth risk.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: XRP at $1.41 despite strongest-ever regulatory tailwinds. Extreme Fear persisting. If improved fundamentals can't lift price, the market may be pricing in a broader crypto derisking cycle.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Currently the lowest risk in the bear case. SEC, OCC, Fed, FDIC all moving in the same pro-crypto direction simultaneously. A reversal would require a political regime change.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 4.09%, Fed stance unclear. BOJ rate hike risk at March meeting would tighten global liquidity. No imminent Fed hike signal but the path to cuts keeps getting delayed.

bear_case.bear_narrative: The XRP thesis stands on a foundation it cannot verify. Two kill switches escalated to WARNING: the ODL data vacuum (12+ months, classified SUSPICIOUS_ABSENCE) and RLUSD pace insufficiency ($1.59B of $5B target, 78% off-XRPL). The institutional signal is paradoxically strong — 7 ETF issuers committed $1.4B with fiduciary liability, SBI deploying real products — while competing infrastructure builders (SWIFT, Chainlink, Circle) are systematically building around XRPL, not through it. INCREASE_MONITORING. Compound stress is MONITORING but pre-loaded: Brent crude at $84.43 is $0.57 from escalating to ELEVATED with energy now the proximate risk vector, not JGB yields. The core paradox remains: the market is pricing institutional settlement utility that the entity most responsible for delivering it has made impossible to verify.
